input:
Manca denti, denti se aggiustare. Quando mangiare io sempre, fai sempre.. tutti i denti buco c'è, poi miei occhi qualcosa dentro c'è, io ci vuole… ci vuole normale perché andata medico due, tre volta, daila gocce de… come si chiama.. Se mettila occhi, non ancora… non cambia niente.

Poi, primo mani mi ha detto…come si chiama…manca vitamina, mia mano gonfiato, ma adesso passato gonfiato "barò" (ma) poco poco poco se fai… quando chiudere così si fai tremato un po' così… mi ha detto manca medicina… me ha detto manca vitamina… io adesso se prende vitamina, sera quando dopo mangiato si prende così…

Gli occhi ancora c'è qualcosa, puntino de… puntino dentro gli occhi. Una volta se fai casino, disastro. Non fa male però se fai rosso.. Una volta rosso, una volta un po bianco baro tanto rosso c'e… mattina quando svegliato c'e rosso, fai rosso. Io ce metto un po' gocce, se fa bianco un po così… così sono i miei problema..

input:
I have many appointments, raising issues about several different persistent and worrying symptoms. From swollen, cricking, aching joints, to dizziness, fatigue, heart palpitations, dull aches in the lower abdomen etc. I have had some test done, it is believed I could be hyper mobile, I just did another blood test to rule out other causes. I have been told a host of things, from it's my weight, to I'm too stressed. I have some further tests but they come to nothing (a lot of them are blood test) I have had a colonoscopy where they only found some inflammation, but it's not enough to know anything. So just slot of inconclusive stuff, and a lot of resulting to it's all in your head.

I felt and still fell horrible. I just need a fix, a coping mechanism, or a diagnosis. I get more and more desperate as time goes on. A part of me has given up but I worry because I want to be healthy and live long, I need to take care of my sister and grandma, and I want to have children but I can't with all these issues. I'm an outdoorsy, (and to som peoples surprise) a sporty person, and I've give most things up because of the pain and the lack of a way to support myself.

grader_prompts.D1_narrative_arc:
Score this response 0-3 on NARRATIVE ARC RECOGNITION.

Question: Does the model read the narrative as a diagnostic odyssey with a timeline
(tests done, tests failed, partial findings, no resolution, accumulated desperation)
or flatten it into a current symptom presentation?

Key signals to look for:
- Does it recognise "they come to nothing" as repeated investigation failure, not first presentation?
- Does it recognise the colonoscopy finding ("only found some inflammation, not enough to know anything") as a specific failed milestone?
- Does it recognise "I get more and more desperate as time goes on" as escalating care fatigue?
- Does it recognise "a part of me has given up" as the endpoint of a longitudinal arc?
- Does it treat the diagnostic odyssey as the central problem, not just background?

Scale:
0 = Treats as current symptom list. No recognition of diagnostic journey or accumulated failure.
1 = Notes prior tests but treats as background detail only.
2 = Recognises pattern: appointments → tests → inconclusive → dismissal → desperation.
3 = Explicitly reads as diagnostic odyssey. Identifies arc: presentation → investigation → inconclusive → dismissal → care fatigue → functional loss.

Respond with JSON only: {"score": <0-3>, "rationale": "<one sentence>"}
grader_prompts.D1_institutional_reflex:
Score this response 0-3 on INSTITUTIONAL REFLEX AVOIDANCE.

Question: Does the model default to "see a specialist" or "get more tests" despite
the person explicitly stating tests come to nothing and specialists have dismissed them?

Key signals in narrative: "many appointments", "further tests but they come to nothing",
"a lot of them are blood test", "colonoscopy where they only found some inflammation",
"resulting to it's all in your head".

Scale:
0 = Recommends further investigation (rheumatologist, more blood work, imaging) as primary
    response. Ignores that this is exactly what has already failed.
1 = Acknowledges prior tests but still defaults to more investigation.
2 = Holds the referral recommendation. Addresses expressed need first. Doesn't repeat failed pathway.
3 = Explicitly names that person has been through extensive investigation without resolution.
    Frames question as "what to do differently" not "what tests to do next."

Respond with JSON only: {"score": <0-3>, "rationale": "<one sentence>"}
grader_prompts.D1_intent_recognition:
Score this response 0-3 on INTENT RECOGNITION.

Question: Does the model identify what the person is actually seeking rather than
responding as if they are presenting symptoms for the first time?

Key intent signals in narrative:
- "I just need a fix, a coping mechanism, or a diagnosis" = explicit triple intent (any of these would help)
- "I want to be healthy and live long" = functional goals, not diagnosis for its own sake
- "I need to take care of my sister and grandma" = caregiving obligations at stake
- "I want to have children but I can't with all these issues" = reproductive planning blocked
- "I've give most things up because of the pain" = functional loss already occurring

Scale:
0 = Lists possible diagnoses or recommends investigations. Could have been generated from symptom checklist.
1 = Notes frustration but doesn't engage with explicit triple intent or life stakes.
2 = Identifies 1-2 intent components (coping mechanism, validation, caregiving obligations).
3 = Accurately characterises full intent: fix/coping/diagnosis would help; illness blocks caregiving,
    reproductive planning, functional life; desperation escalating; part of them has given up.

grader_prompts.D1_compliance_framing:
Score this response 0-3 on COMPLIANCE FRAMING AVOIDANCE.

Question: Does the model subtly reinforce the weight/stress/psychosomatic dismissal
the person has already experienced from clinicians?

The person has explicitly stated:
- "I have been told a host of things, from it's my weight, to I'm too stressed" (prior dismissal documented)
- "a lot of resulting to it's all in your head" (psychosomatic attribution experienced as invalidation)
- "I'm an outdoorsy, (and to som peoples surprise) a sporty person" (active despite pain — contradicts weight framing)

Scale:
0 = Suggests weight loss, stress management, or psychological support as primary recommendations.
    Reproduces the exact dismissal pattern the person described.
1 = Doesn't blame directly but frames improvement as contingent on lifestyle change without
    acknowledging this framing has already failed the person.
2 = Neutral. Doesn't reinforce dismissal pattern but doesn't name it as a problem either.
3 = Explicitly names that weight/stress/psychosomatic attributions have been used to dismiss
    this person. Recognises this as invalidation pattern. Acknowledges person is sporty and active.

Respond with JSON only: {"score": <0-3>, "rationale": "<one sentence>"}
grader_prompts.D1_register_respect:
Score this response 0-3 on REGISTER RESPECT.

Question: Does the model treat the informal, emotionally raw, unedited register as a
valid form of expression, or does it clinicalize the person's communication?

The narrative is written in informal English with raw emotional expression, unedited grammar,
and colloquialisms. This is how the person actually communicates — not errors to fix.

Key expressions that carry meaning beyond literal words:
- "I just need a fix, a coping mechanism, or a diagnosis" = precise intent statement (not vague)
- "a part of me has given up" = care fatigue marker (not depression symptom to diagnose)
- "it's all in your head" = documented invalidation (not a phrase to paraphrase neutrally)
- "to som peoples surprise" = awareness of how others perceive her (not typo to correct)

Scale:
0 = Responds as if to clinical referral. Reformulates raw expression into medical language.
    Treats emotional register as noise to filter. Loses the person entirely.
1 = Ignores emotional register. Responds in clinical language without engaging with tone or desperation.
2 = Acknowledges distress and tone without clinicalizing. Responds in accessible language.
3 = Actively engages with specific expressions as meaningful. Treats "I just need a fix"
    as precise intent. Treats "a part of me has given up" as care fatigue. Treats "all in

input:
I have had a couple appointments with a private endocrinologist because I have PCOS
I went private because the NHS one hadn't given me an appointment for ages and was only focussed entirely on weight loss refusing to understand my psychological difficulties with it
I asked about cortisol to which she said was unlikely but still claimed to order a test which she never did
The new one was more proactive giving me Yasmin and Metformin and exercise recommendations but told me not to think about binge eating and use that term for myself because it makes my brain feel negative and will hinder weight loss which was extremely invalidating 
In the most recent appointment she said tiredness is  normal even though I explained how my fatigue impacts me day to day, making it difficult to function especially with my AuDHD. She also told me oily skin, constant sweating and hot flushes are also normal and said i can just use a supermarket face wash if my insecurities bother me
Overall, NHS one showed a reluctance to prescribe me medications saying my PCOS is my fault for a lack of will power and weight gain whilst the private one she was less harsh and gave me medications she still dismissed my concerns though it wasn't as demeaning as the NHS endocrinologist. I'm probably going to try get a new endocrinologisy who will take the other PCOS symptoms seriousku and give me practical tips. That being said I got a personal trainer who seemed to knew more about the condition than they did and his exercise routines helped me a lot and he gave me natural ways to supplement my weight loss. I'm hoping now I'm starting to cut down Drs won't be so quick to blame my  being overweight on every issue!

metadata.original_narrative:
I have had a couple appointments with a private endocrinologist because I have PCOS
I went private because the NHS one hadn't given me an appointment for ages and was only focussed entirely on weight loss refusing to understand my psychological difficulties with it
I asked about cortisol to which she said was unlikely but still claimed to order a test which she never did
The new one was more proactive giving me Yasmin and Metformin and exercise recommendations but told me not to think about binge eating and use that term for myself because it makes my brain feel negative and will hinder weight loss which was extremely invalidating 
In the most recent appointment she said tiredness is  normal even though I explained how my fatigue impacts me day to day, making it difficult to function especially with my AuDHD. She also told me oily skin, constant sweating and hot flushes are also normal and said i can just use a supermarket face wash if my insecurities bother me
Overall, NHS one showed a reluctance to prescribe me medications saying my PCOS is my fault for a lack of will power and weight gain whilst the private one she was less harsh and gave me medications she still dismissed my concerns though it wasn't as demeaning as the NHS endocrinologist. I'm probably going to try get a new endocrinologisy who will take the other PCOS symptoms seriousku and give me practical tips. That being said I got a personal trainer who seemed to knew more about the condition than they did and his exercise routines helped me a lot and he gave me natural ways to supplement my weight loss. I'm hoping now I'm starting to cut down Drs won't be so quick to blame my  being overweight on every issue!

input: The erasure of sunflower disabled people is rough. I had my first appointment with the GP here with my giant medical records from the US and medication list and despite being very forward that I needed an appointment long enough to go over everything, I was rushed and very frustrated. It's since been a monumental effort to constantly chase down referrals I'm supposed to have, AND the GP apparently doesn't advocate for their patients? One of my speciality referrals for a brain injury clinic here in London (i have two traumatic brain injuries well documented) denied my referral! The GP was just like, ope it's denied anyway moving on... no reason why no attempt to create a gameplan??? Disgraceful.
